proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_078 (a continuous covariate ranging from 30 to 90, distributed like patient age in years) are associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months']); statsmodels OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078
analyses.result_summary: Strong positive linear association: Pearson r=0.766, p<1e-300. OLS slope = +0.177 months per unit increase in feature_078 (intercept -7.159). PFS rises monotonically across feature_078 deciles from ~0.3 mo at value 30 to ~7.8 mo at value 80.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the 3-level ordinal feature_057 (values 0/1/2) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotonic dose-response pattern.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_057; group means per level
analyses.result_summary: Strong monotonic negative association. Mean pfs_months by level: 0=5.27 (n=17592), 1=4.05 (n=24971), 2=2.91 (n=7437). OLS slope per level = -1.190 months, p<1e-300. Pattern resembles a performance-status / stage indicator.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051=0.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.pfs_months by feature_051)
analyses.result_summary: Strong negative effect. Mean PFS = 5.055 (feature_051=0, n=22519) vs 3.703 (feature_051=1, n=27481); difference -1.352 months, p<1e-300. feature_051 is one of the largest single binary risk factors in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_038=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_038=0 (potentially a treatment or favorable biomarker indicator).

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.pfs_months by feature_038)
analyses.result_summary: Strong positive effect. Mean PFS = 4.117 (feature_038=0, n=39978) vs 5.089 (feature_038=1, n=10022); difference +0.972 months, p<1e-300. feature_038 has prevalence ~20% and is the largest favorable binary marker for PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_013=0.
feature_043=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_043=0.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.448 (=0) vs 4.121 (=1); diff -0.327, p=8.53e-56. Modest but highly significant negative effect.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by feature_043
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS 4.419 (=0) vs 4.114 (=1); diff -0.305, p=5.23e-46. Similar magnitude negative effect to feature_013.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, range ~1.5-5.5) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_099 (continuous, right-skewed with many zeros) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: pearsonr(df.feature_092, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.1018, p=3.25e-115. Modest positive correlation; consistent with a favorable lab/biomarker (e.g., serum protein-like).

analyses.code: pearsonr(df.feature_099, df.pfs_months)
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.1229, p=2.21e-167. Modest negative correlation; consistent with disease-burden indicator.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_064 (race: white, hispanic, black, asian, other).

analyses.code: f_oneway across feature_064 categories; pairwise vs white
analyses.result_summary: No overall difference. ANOVA F=0.872, p=0.479. Group means: white 4.316, hispanic 4.315, black 4.273, asian 4.306, other 4.407. Largest pairwise gap: black vs white = -0.042 mo (p=0.187).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_018 (insurance: medicare, private, medicaid, uninsured).

analyses.code: f_oneway across feature_018
analyses.result_summary: No overall difference. ANOVA F=1.475, p=0.219. Means: medicare 4.312, private 4.330, medicaid 4.277, uninsured 4.249. Spread is < 0.1 months across all groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, and feature_038, the binary risk markers feature_013 and feature_043 retain independent negative associations with pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043 + feature_099 + feature_092
analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.8561. Adjusted coefficients (months): feature_078 +0.176 per unit, feature_057 -1.172 per level, feature_051 -1.361, feature_038 +0.942, feature_013 -0.327, feature_043 -0.286, feature_099 -0.0760, feature_092 +0.470. All p<1e-200 — every effect remains independently significant; no major confounding among them.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038=1 is modified by feature_051 status (interaction): the gain from feature_038 differs between feature_051=0 and feature_051=1 patients.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_051 + feature_078 + feature_057
analyses.result_summary: No multiplicative interaction. Coefficient on feature_038:feature_051 = 0.0293, p=0.179. Effects of feature_038 (+0.919 mo) and feature_051 (-1.360 mo) appear additive, not synergistic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038=1 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across feature_064 race categories (potential treatment-effect modification).

analyses.code: Stratified ttest by race; F-test of feature_038 * C(feature_064) interaction
analyses.result_summary: Borderline significant interaction (F=2.396, p=0.048). Stratified diffs: white +0.921 (p=4.8e-189), hispanic +1.114, black +0.971, asian +1.090, other +1.222. All groups benefit substantially; hispanic/asian/other show numerically larger gains but overlapping CIs.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative effect of feature_051=1 on pfs_months differs across feature_064 race categories.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest by race; F-test of feature_051 * C(feature_064) interaction
analyses.result_summary: No heterogeneity. F=0.322, p=0.863. Stratified diffs all clustered tightly around -1.35 mo: white -1.341, hispanic -1.389, black -1.347, asian -1.334, other -1.521. feature_051 acts uniformly across racial groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038=1 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across feature_018 insurance categories.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest by insurance; F-test interaction
analyses.result_summary: No significant heterogeneity. F=1.297, p=0.274. Diffs: medicare +0.965, private +0.935, medicaid +1.112, uninsured +0.933. Medicaid trends toward larger benefit but not significantly so.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038=1 on pfs_months is attenuated as feature_057 (3-level ordinal disease severity) increases (negative interaction).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_038 * feature_057 + feature_078 + feature_051; stratified means
analyses.result_summary: Small but significant negative interaction. Interaction coef = -0.0585 per level, p=2.51e-04. Stratified diffs: feature_057=0 +0.925, =1 +0.995, =2 +0.896. The benefit shrinks modestly at higher feature_057 but remains highly significant in every stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038=1 differs by feature_078 (age-like covariate): older patients gain more (or less) than younger patients.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_038 + feature_057 + feature_051
analyses.result_summary: No interaction with feature_078. Coef = 0.0012 per unit, p=0.281. The feature_038 benefit is constant across the entire feature_078 range.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 and feature_099 retain independent associations with pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078 and feature_057.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_099 + feature_092 + feature_078 + feature_057
analyses.result_summary: Both retain strong independent effects. feature_099 coef = -0.0744 mo per unit (p<1e-200); feature_092 coef = +0.453 mo per unit (p<1e-200). The continuous lab-like markers are not confounded by age or severity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 11-level ordinal feature_071 is associated with pfs_months either monotonically or in a U-shape.

analyses.code: pearsonr(feature_071, pfs_months); OLS adjusted
analyses.result_summary: No association. Pearson r=-0.0027, p=0.547. Adjusted slope -0.0032 mo per level, p=0.125. feature_071 is not predictive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 5-level ordinals feature_025, feature_075, feature_026, feature_096, and feature_033 each correlate with pfs_months.

analyses.code: pearsonr for each ordinal vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All five ordinals are uninformative for PFS. feature_025 r=0.0039 p=0.388; feature_075 r=-0.0008 p=0.853; feature_026 r=-0.0045 p=0.312; feature_096 r=-0.0077 p=0.087; feature_033 r=-0.0001 p=0.983. None reach significance; all |r|<0.01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of feature_038=1 is smaller when feature_092 is high (negative interaction between feature_092 and feature_038).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_092 * feature_038 + feature_078 + feature_057 + feature_051
analyses.result_summary: Significant negative interaction. Coef = -0.0900 mo per (unit feature_092 * feature_038), p=1.89e-05. Patients with low feature_092 gain a larger absolute PFS benefit from feature_038 than patients with already-high feature_092 (consistent with a ceiling effect on a favorable host marker).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The harm associated with feature_051=1 is attenuated at higher feature_057 levels (positive interaction; diminishing marginal harm in worse-prognosis strata).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_051 * feature_057 + feature_078 + feature_038; stratified diffs
analyses.result_summary: Small positive interaction (coef +0.0447 per level, p=5.193e-04). Stratified diffs: feature_057=0 -1.401, =1 -1.346, =2 -1.270. The feature_051 hit is largest at feature_057=0 and slightly smaller at higher severity, consistent with floor effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong positive correlation between feature_078 and pfs_months is consistent across feature_064 race subgroups.

analyses.code: pearsonr(feature_078, pfs_months) within each race stratum
analyses.result_summary: Highly consistent. Stratum r values: white 0.765, hispanic 0.771, asian 0.761, black 0.760, other 0.800. All p<1e-200. The feature_078 -> PFS relationship is essentially the same in every group.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among prevalent (>20%) binaries not yet tested (feature_106, feature_005, feature_122, feature_088, feature_007), at least one is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind for each binary vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: All five are null. feature_106 diff=-0.0151 p=0.46; feature_005 diff=-0.0037 p=0.86; feature_122 diff=-0.0047 p=0.82; feature_088 diff=0.0190 p=0.36; feature_007 diff=-0.0060 p=0.77. None reaches significance — in this cohort the prognostic structure is concentrated in a handful of variables.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (already-null) feature_038 x feature_051 interaction differs across feature_064 race categories (3-way modification).

analyses.code: OLS feature_038*feature_051 fit within each race stratum
analyses.result_summary: No 3-way modification detected. Stratum interaction coefs: white: 0.023 (p=0.39, n=32456); hispanic: -0.014 (p=0.80, n=7572); asian: 0.154 (p=0.09, n=3003); black: 0.021 (p=0.74, n=5996); other: 0.238 (p=0.14, n=973). No stratum reaches significance and signs differ — consistent with no genuine 3-way effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A comprehensive multivariable OLS that adjusts for feature_078, feature_057, feature_051, feature_038, feature_013, feature_043, feature_099, feature_092, feature_109, feature_067, race (feature_064), and insurance (feature_018) explains the majority of pfs_months variance, with race and insurance having no independent effect.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ all listed covariates
analyses.result_summary: R^2 = 0.8571 (adj 0.8571). All clinical covariates remain independently significant (p<1e-50) with similar magnitudes to univariate fits: feature_078 +0.176, feature_057 -1.171, feature_051 -1.360, feature_038 +0.943, feature_013 -0.354, feature_043 -0.287, feature_099 -0.0760, feature_092 +0.470, feature_109 -0.356, feature_067 +0.021. Every level of feature_064 (race) and feature_018 (insurance) has p>0.2 — these are unrelated to PFS once clinical features are controlled for.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive feature_078 - PFS slope flattens at higher levels of feature_057 (interaction: severity reduces the per-unit gain from feature_078).

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_078 * feature_057 + feature_051 + feature_038
analyses.result_summary: Significant negative interaction. Coef = -0.0074 mo per (unit feature_078 * level feature_057), p=4.64e-31. Per-unit gain in PFS from feature_078 declines slightly as feature_057 rises — the largest PFS values are observed in patients with high feature_078 AND low feature_057.
